title: Dunkirk
logline: During the 1940 Dunkirk evacuation, a young British soldier fights to survive on the beach, a civilian sailor crosses the Channel in his small boat, and an RAF pilot defends the operation from the air—three timelines converging in a desperate race against annihilation.
antagonistOrConflict: Time, the encroaching German forces, and the sea itself
tone: Relentless, visceral, claustrophobic—minimal dialogue, maximum tension through sound and image
themes: Home as both destination and illusion

actBreaks.description: All three timelines are now in motion and converging toward Dunkirk—the mole empties, the Moonstone commits to the crossing, and Farrier engages the enemy.

validation.ruling: Scene #40 fundamentally shifts the story as the Rear Admiral reveals Britain itself is next if the army isn't evacuated, transforming the stakes from local survival to preventing global Nazi domination—an irreversible escalation that propels all three timelines into higher-stakes action.

actBreaks.description: The grounded trawler sequence climaxes with paranoia and betrayal as Gibson is revealed as French; simultaneously, Collins ditches and the timelines begin their final convergence.

validation.ruling: Scene #128 delivers a major character revelation and moral crisis as Gibson is exposed as French, creating irreversible division among the trapped soldiers while machine-gun fire floods the trawler—a climactic turning point that forces immediate life-or-death decisions and propels the story into its final act.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates on three asynchronous timelines—one week on the mole, one day on the sea, one hour in the air—that only reveal their temporal relationship through careful cross-cutting. It's not non-linear in the Tarantino sense; it's architecturally precise, like three separate clocks ticking at different speeds until they all strike the same hour. The 'acts' here aren't traditional—Act 1 establishes the three separate survival mechanisms (waiting, sailing, fighting), Act 2 escalates each to breaking point, and Act 3 synchronizes them into a single climactic convergence where all three protagonists occupy the same moment. The structure itself IS the theme: individual experience fragments under trauma, but collective action creates meaning. Nolan withholds the temporal trick until the audience intuitively grasps it, making the structure feel discovered rather than imposed.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is relentless but asymmetrical. The mole sequences (scenes 8-15, 40, 46, etc.) stretch time through waiting and repetition—Tommy's survival is measured in failed escape attempts. The sea sequences accelerate gradually as the Moonstone moves from departure (scene 16) through mounting crises (the Shivering Soldier, George's injury) to frantic rescue operations (scenes 150-175). The air sequences are pure compression—Farrier's fuel gauge breaks early (scene 30), and every subsequent scene is a countdown to empty. The script uses scene length as a pacing tool: air scenes average 0.3 pages (quick cuts, dogfight rhythm), sea scenes average 1.2 pages (building dread), mole scenes vary wildly (long waits punctuated by sudden violence). The final 15 pages (scenes 177-197) intercut all three timelines at maximum velocity, creating a rhythmic crescendo that feels like three separate films colliding.

incitingIncident.description: Tommy emerges onto a vast beach crowded with hundreds of thousands of soldiers in lines stretching into the water, squats to relieve himself, then helps Gibson bury a body and tie boots taken from the corpse.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: EXT. BEACH AT MALO LES BAINS - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #1 shatters Tommy's ordinary world as he arrives at Dunkirk beach to discover hundreds of thousands of stranded soldiers in impossibly long lines waiting for ships that don't move, establishing the main survival conflict and irreversible stakes of the evacuation.

midpoint.description: Tommy and Alex swim through burning wreckage and bodies; they grab at an overloaded rowboat but are pushed off; a soldier in the boat—the same man who will later become the Shivering Soldier—warns them they'll capsize it.
midpoint.sceneHeading: EXT. WATER, JUST OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - NIGHT

validation.ruling: Scene #91 is a reactive survival moment (swimming after torpedo). Scene #94 at 49% marks the true midpoint: Farrier's fuel gauge breaks during combat, forcing him into a proactive point-of-no-return decision to continue the mission blind on fuel, significantly raising personal stakes and transforming him from reactive defender to committed protector.

climax.description: Commander Bolton mumbles a prayer as the Stuka comes at them, then Spitfire 1 flashes past and strafes the bomber with gunfire.

validation.ruling: Scene #169 is a brief action beat (guns blazing). Scene #168 is the true climax: Commander Bolton prays as the Stuka dives at the mole in peak tension, then Farrier's Spitfire flashes past and strafes the bomber, demonstrating his arc completion by saving the soldiers despite being out of fuel—the ultimate confrontation resolving the air battle storyline.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT MALO LES BAINS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy emerges onto a vast beach crowded with hundreds of thousands of soldiers in lines stretching into the water, squats to relieve himself, then helps Gibson bury a body and tie boots taken from the corpse.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tommy reaches Dunkirk beach and witnesses the massive scale of the evacuation for the first time, establishing the central survival challenge.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENGLISH COAST, WEYMOUTH HARBOUR - MORNING
scenes.summary: George runs to the Moonstone yacht where Mr. Dawson and Peter are loading life vests as naval officers requisition boats in the harbor.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY - DAY
scenes.summary: Three Spitfires fly in formation through the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier checks his fuel gauge which shows seventy gallons and receives radio instructions to stay at five hundred feet.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins reports sixty-eight gallons of fuel and confirms the flight vector and altitude over radio.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier checks his chart, receives a reminder to watch his fuel gauge, and pulls on the stick.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The three Spitfires bank left in perfect formation.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT LA PANNE - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Tommy finds wounded soldiers on stretchers with one still alive, and he and Gibson grab a stretcher and rush toward the mole.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE OF THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A Warrant Officer directs the line at the mole's base, waves stretcher-bearers past when a ship's whistle sounds, and orders men to make way.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson hear the ship's whistle and start running with their stretcher toward the mole.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE OF THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Warrant Officer turns away French soldiers, tells Tommy and Gibson they've missed the departing ship, but Tommy pushes forward with the stretcher anyway as the officer shakes his head.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL SHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A Petty Officer supervises stretchers being loaded onto the hospital ship, checks his watch, and looks at the remaining stretchers along the mole.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy weaves along the crowded mole, leans over the edge where the rail is missing above a twenty-foot drop, and Gibson follows his route.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL SHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The last stretcher is loaded onto the ship, the Petty Officer confirms it's the last one, then an explosion hits the water nearby and everyone hits the deck.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: An ME 109 strafes the mole with gunfire, soldiers hit the deck and several are hit, Gibson stumbles then struggles back up.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEYMOUTH HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter and George load life-preservers while Mr. Dawson watches naval officers approaching along the harbor assigning crew to boats.

scenes.heading: INT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter bursts into the cabin stacking life vests.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson watches the naval men coming closer as the pile of life vests on the dock shrinks.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier lightly touches the controls and responds to Collins' question about Calais by relaying that the enemy prevented loading there.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins scans the skies and comments they're sitting ducks, receiving a warning to watch for enemy planes coming out of the sun.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier looks around into the blinding sun.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL SHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Petty Officer orders the crew to prepare departure while troops on the mole below look up resentfully, one soldier asking if there's more room.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy reaches a jagged chasm with one narrow plank across a fifteen-foot drop, the ship's whistle sounds, and he and Gibson bolt across the bouncing plank with Tommy's foot slipping before helpful arms grab them on the other side.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL SHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson push past returning stretcher-bearers, the Petty Officer orders gangplanks pulled, Tommy shouts and a gangplank is shoved back down, they struggle up with the stretcher and drop it on deck as orderlies take it below.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tommy and Gibson successfully board the hospital ship, achieving their immediate goal of escape from the beach.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson starts the engine as naval officers approach, George unties the stern line and hesitates, Peter declares he and his father are going, and George jumps aboard as the boat moves off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mr. Dawson defies the navy's requisition and sets out for Dunkirk with his son and George, committing to the rescue mission.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins spots something in the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier spots an ME 109 coming out of the sun and calls out its position, receiving the order to break formation.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY OVER ENGLISH CHANNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The three Spitfires dart away from each other as the German plane pursues Collins and hurtles down.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins dives and rolls, glancing back and calling out that the enemy is on him.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier banks around and lines up on Collins' pursuer, announcing he's on the enemy plane.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOSPITAL SHIP - EVENING
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson shuffle around the deck looking for a spot when an Able Seaman orders them off, and Tommy reluctantly follows Gibson onto the gangplank.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy looks at the thousands queuing on the mole, a Second Lieutenant orders them back up the line, Gibson hides in the structure below the mole and beckons Tommy to join him, and Tommy slips down beside Gibson as officers discuss rudder damage above.
scenes.turningPointNote: Tommy and Gibson hide beneath the mole to avoid being sent back, choosing to stay on the ship illegally rather than return to the beach.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - MORNING
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson steers from outside with Peter at his side while George on the bow spots a bomber overhead, which Mr. Dawson identifies as friendly, and George observes the growing convoy of vessels heading toward Dunkirk.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier concentrates on angling his plane at the ME 109's tail as the German plane evades, and he coordinates with Collins to draw the enemy left.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins pulls hard left and rolls as tracer fire streaks past.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier watches the ME 109 cut left to follow Collins, fires his cannons, and smoke starts trailing from the German plane.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY OVER ENGLISH CHANNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ME 109 trails heavy smoke as it tips toward the water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins straightens out and tries to look back, asking if the enemy is down.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier watches the ME 109 smash into the water, reports the kill, then tracer fire smashes into his plane from a second 109, he banks hard right, tries to contact Fortis Leader with no response, spots Collins and orders an orbit to search, then discovers his fuel gauge is broken.
scenes.turningPointNote: Farrier's plane is hit and his fuel gauge is destroyed, creating a critical handicap for the remainder of his mission, and Fortis Leader is lost.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson hide beneath the mole listening as Commander Bolton, Colonel Winnant, and the Rear Admiral discuss evacuation priorities, the shrinking perimeter, and the decision to prioritize fighting men over wounded, with the Rear Admiral estimating they can save thirty thousand men before the week is out.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson approaches an overturned wreck surrounded by bodies, throttles back, and Peter asks a shivering soldier crouched on the hull if he can swim, but the soldier refuses to go back in the water and is pulled aboard.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY ABOVE THE ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The two Spitfires arc around the wreckage of the ME 109.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier spots wreckage below, identifies it as Fortis Leader's plane, reports no parachute was seen, records the position, sets a new heading, then discovers his fuel gauge glass is shattered.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins checks his fuel gauge and reports fifty gallons.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier notes Collins' fuel reading with a grease pencil, informs Collins his gauge is broken, Collins suggests he turn back, but Farrier insists he's confident it's just the gauge and sets his watch bezel before tapping the gauge again with no result.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Bolton watches the Rear Admiral depart as the dreaded sound of Stukas builds, soldiers on the exposed mole look up with nowhere to go, the bombers dive, and bombs impact the sea on either side of the mole as soldiers crouch and the onslaught continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Shivering Soldier sits in the well with a blanket, refuses George's offer to go below, smacks George's arm away when touched, and reveals he was torpedoed by a U-boat not bombed.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY OVER ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The two Spitfires head toward the massive black smoke over Dunkirk with many ships and boats visible in the water ahead.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins checks his fuel gauge and reports forty gallons to Farrier.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier instinctively looks at his broken gauge, notes Collins' fuel reading with grease pencil, orders a climb to two thousand feet despite Collins' concern about fuel consumption, explaining he wants altitude to dive on enemy planes, and pulls back on the stick.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY OVER ENGLISH CHANNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Spitfires ascend into higher altitude over the English Channel.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton directs wet soldiers to another ship; Tommy and Gibson drench themselves to blend in with the Highlanders, and Alex helps them join the group boarding a launch.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCH - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson hide among the Highlanders on the launch as it approaches a destroyer, where men struggle to climb cargo nets up the bobbing iron hull.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Exhausted men collapse on the destroyer's deck as sailors and nurses urge them below, where a nurse promises tea.

scenes.heading: INT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy descends into the crowded hold while Gibson refuses to go below and steps back from the stairwell; Alex follows Tommy and they receive tea and bread.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gibson sits alone on deck in the gathering darkness as the ship begins moving.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Alex eat hungrily below deck; when Alex asks about Gibson, Tommy explains he's looking for a quick escape route in case the ship sinks, then they move toward the stairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Shivering Soldier realizes they're heading to Dunkirk and violently refuses to return, insisting they'll die if they go back.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter sits the Shivering Soldier down in the forepeak cabin and considers bolting the door shut.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: When George asks if the soldier is a coward, Mr. Dawson sharply corrects him, explaining the man is shell-shocked and may never recover.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Peter gives the Shivering Soldier tea, then quietly slides the bolt to lock him inside the cabin.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier spots a German Heinkel bomber lining up to attack a minesweeper packed with troops and asks Collins about fighter escorts.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins identifies German 109 fighters escorting the bomber; Farrier announces he'll attack the bomber while Collins engages the fighters.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY OVER ENGLISH CHANNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Both Spitfires dive with cannons firing—Collins at the German fighters, Farrier at the bomber.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier fires on the Heinkel, then rolls away beneath it and pulls out of his dive while fighting g-forces.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins fires at a 109 until it trails smoke, then dives between the German planes.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier lines up another attack run from below, fires on the bomber, and flashes past its top turret which shoots tracer bullets at him.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins confirms the Heinkel is turning away damaged, but cannon fire suddenly rips into his Spitfire, setting it ablaze; he reports he's going down and begins preparing to bail out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Collins is shot down, forcing him to abandon his plane and leaving Farrier alone in the air

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Soldiers in the packed hold cheer as the destroyer's engines start and the ship begins moving.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gibson watches rowboats shouting for the ship to wait when he spots a torpedo wake; someone shouts a warning and an explosion lifts water at the ship's side.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The cheering stops as massive explosions shudder the iron walls of the hold.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A blast rocks every bolt of the destroyer.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Everyone standing is thrown off their feet inside the hold.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Fire explodes from the funnel and the deck blasts apart.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Men scream as the hold's iron walls buckle and water blasts in; the lights go out and screaming is barely audible over rushing water and bending metal.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gibson prepares to jump from the rapidly sinking ship but turns back to open the door to the hold.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy spots Gibson waving in the doorway's dim light and he and Alex claw their way up the steps as the ship goes under.

scenes.heading: EXT. DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Alex burst through the door as it sinks beneath the waves and swim desperately away from the disappearing ship.
scenes.turningPointNote: The destroyer is torpedoed and sinks, trapping hundreds below deck while Tommy, Alex, and Gibson barely escape into the water

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: George looks up at three Spitfires passing overhead; Mr. Dawson identifies them by their Rolls Royce Merlin engine sound without looking.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter hears the Shivering Soldier rattling and banging on the locked forepeak door, demanding to be let out; Peter freezes, uncertain, then turns away.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter tells Mr. Dawson the soldier wants out; Mr. Dawson, hearing the banging, asks if Peter locked him in and orders him to let the man out.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter reluctantly unbolts the door and opens it to find the forepeak empty and the forward hatch open.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Shivering Soldier confronts Mr. Dawson for not turning around, calling him too old for this; Mr. Dawson calmly argues that men his age started the war and there's no hiding from the slaughter across the Channel.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier chases the 109 as it circles back toward Collins.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins checks his burning wing, altimeter, and canopy as he descends.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier fires at the 109 and chases it away from Collins.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins checks his harness, Mae West, and fuel gauge reading fifteen gallons as he continues descending toward the water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier marks Collins's fuel reading of fifteen gallons on his chart.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins checks wind and wave direction as he descends, lines up along the waves, and ditches his burning Spitfire on the water's surface.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier watches Collins ditch successfully and float; he sees a civilian yacht heading for Collins, spots Collins waving, tips his wings in acknowledgment, and turns to chase the 109 toward Dunkirk.

scenes.heading: EXT. WATER, JUST OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tommy and Alex swim through burning wreckage and bodies; they grab at an overloaded rowboat but are pushed off; a soldier in the boat—the same man who will later become the Shivering Soldier—warns them they'll capsize it.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - DAY
scenes.summary: The Shivering Soldier confronts Mr. Dawson, calling him an old fool who will die without guns; Mr. Dawson asks if the soldier's rifle helped against dive bombers or U-boats.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: George lies bleeding from the back of his head at the foot of the cabin steps; Peter puts a life jacket behind his head and tries to comfort him.
scenes.turningPointNote: George is seriously injured, bleeding from the head after an apparent confrontation with the Shivering Soldier

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier chases the 109 toward a convoy near the harbour entrance, passes over a sinking trawler covered with soldiers, fires at the 109, then checks his broken fuel gauge and realizes he has no reading.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT ZYDECOTTE (7 MILES EAST OF DUNKIRK) - DAWN
scenes.summary: At dawn on a stormy beach seven miles east of Dunkirk, Tommy, Gibson, and Alex sleep while nearby soldiers struggle with swamped rowboats and overcrowded vessels in the surf.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT MALO LES BAINS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Colonel Winnant surveys the beach where engineers build a makeshift pier from trucks; an engineer quietly notes the turning tide brings the bodies back, and Winnant watches soldiers push floating corpses aside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT ZYDECOTTE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy and Gibson share a tin of vegetables; Alex spots Highlanders and follows them to discover a beached trawler they plan to float when the tide comes in.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter checks George's bleeding and asks why he came along; George desperately explains he wanted to do something to make his school proud and get in the local paper.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter tells Mr. Dawson that George's bleeding won't stop and asks if they should turn back; Mr. Dawson looks both ways, decides they've come too far, and refuses as explosions boom nearby.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier glances between his broken fuel gauge and reserve tank switch, curses, then banks and climbs to gain altitude for an attack on German planes threatening the destroyer and blue trawler.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE OF THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Colonel Winnant learns from a Private that the French have been forced back on the western side but are still holding the perimeter, then pushes on down the mole toward Bolton.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Winnant discovers from Bolton that only one destroyer at a time will dock due to yesterday's losses, and Bolton reveals they've activated the civilian boats pool for requisition.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of the civilian boats pool marks a strategic shift in the evacuation plan, setting up the arrival of the Little Ships.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson spots a distant Heinkel bomber, throws the wheel hard to starboard, and motors away from a minesweeper he predicts will be the target, telling Peter they must survive to pick up survivors.

scenes.heading: EXT. GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: The Highlanders and Tommy and Gibson approach a grounded blue trawler on a deserted beach and climb aboard the abandoned vessel.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK OF GROUNDED TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alex questions why the crew left, learns they're outside the perimeter with enemy possibly in the dunes, and the Highlanders decide to shut themselves inside the hold to wait for high tide in three hours.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter spots two Spitfires diving at the German bomber and its escort, one Spitfire sets a 109 alight, but then one of the Spitfires starts smoking.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier's engine skips a beat, he puts his finger on the reserve tank toggle switch, the engine evens out, and he throttles up to climb into the fray.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex tells Gibson to check if water is coming in, Gibson refuses, Tommy checks and reports the tide has barely come in at all, and a Highlander tells them to calm down and wait.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson steers the Moonstone at full speed toward the smoke-trailing Spitfire while Peter watches it fly lower and lower without a parachute.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins pulls back on the stick as his Spitfire hits the water with a jolt and tearing sound, thrashing him against his belts.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter watches the Spitfire land on the surface of the water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins releases his belts, inflates his life vest, tries to open the jammed canopy, watches Farrier's Spitfire salute overhead, and realizes he is trapped as water rises around the slowly sinking plane.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier levels off and spots the Heinkel approaching with a single ME 109 fighter escort off its port wing.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy hears steps outside, Highlander 1 aims his rifle at the door, Tommy pulls in a Dutch merchant seaman who explains he came to help pick them up but left the boat because it ran aground outside the perimeter.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson pushes the boat toward where the plane went down despite Peter's protests that there was no parachute, snapping that the pilot might still be alive.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier lines up for his attack and pushes forward on his stick, going into his dive toward the Heinkel.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Bullets punch holes through the hull in a tight grouping, soldiers ease away from the holes, two Highlanders grab rifles to go outside but Tommy stops them, arguing the tight grouping indicates target practice rather than awareness of their presence.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - DAY
scenes.summary: The Moonstone gets close enough to see the Spitfire bobbing on the waves and sinking, and Mr. Dawson tells Peter to go forward with the boat hook.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins smashes the jammed canopy repeatedly, water pours in through the gap and rises past his ankles and calves as he searches desperately for an implement.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier dives toward the Heinkel and glances across to see the 109 banking toward him in reaction to his attack.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: A Highlander tries to plug the bullet holes but gets shot in the face and screams, his comrades pull him back to smother his cries while water pours in steadily through the holes.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins pulls the steel flare gun from its holder and smashes it repeatedly into the canopy as water comes up over his legs.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier fires at the Heinkel with tracers zipping at the bomber, but the 109 rises at him with guns blazing and Farrier rolls away to dodge the fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. SKY ABOVE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: As Spitfire 1 rolls away, the Heinkel releases its bombs which fall around the destroyer.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: A burst of machine gun fire opens new holes, Alex asks the Dutch Seaman if they need to lose weight to get off, and Alex turns to the group declaring someone needs to get off and points at Gibson.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins smacks the canopy with the flare gun which bounces off, drops it, scrambles to find it underwater as the water rises up his chest.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier cuts right to dodge the 109, banks hard, and gets a clear look at the destroyer weathering the explosions with a plume of water rising beside it.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex takes Highlander 1's rifle, hooks Gibson's tags to read them, and Gibson cracks under pressure revealing he is French, prompting Alex to accuse him of being a cowardly queue-jumping Frog who stole a dead Englishman's identity, while Tommy defends Gibson saying he helped bury the body and didn't kill anyone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gibson's true identity as a French soldier is revealed, creating a major character conflict and raising questions about his legitimacy among the British soldiers.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 2 - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins panics as water rises over his ears, banging with his fists until a boat hook smashes through the canopy, and he pulls himself through the hole and pushes up from the sinking plane underwater.

scenes.heading: EXT. WATER, JUST OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins breaks the surface gasping, sees Peter on the Moonstone's bow with the boat hook extended, and grasps it.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier searches the sky for German planes, looks down at the destroyer leaking oil from a large hole in its side, and sees the dark oil slick spreading across the water covering the men between the trawler and destroyer.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton and Colonel Winnant watch the destroyer Basilisk cast off, hear distant shots, and Bolton spots through field glasses a grounded blue trawler taking fire as Winnant observes the enemy is breaking through the dunes to the east.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, GROUNDED TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy comes up for air gasping, water pours in from dozens of holes, Alex surfaces with Gibson and shouts they're off, then climbs out to the stairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alex pokes his head out as the Dutch Seaman turns the wheel and jumps back as bullets impact the cabin, throws the engine into forward gear, and yells at Alex to plug the holes.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Alex falls down the stairs shouting to plug the holes, and the men stuff rags, bolts, fingers, and anything they can find to plug as many holes as possible.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Collins looks down at George whose breathing is shallow with sightless eyes open, and tells Peter he was right not to move him and has done the best he can.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Shivering Soldier asks if George is alright, Peter says no and glares at him, a destroyer ahead is bombed with huge plumes of water rising, Peter spots men in the water from a sinking blue trawler, and Mr. Dawson throttles up toward them.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier lines up behind a 109 and fires, chasing it down until it smokes and drops, but his engine chokes in the heavy dive and he switches to reserve tank before the engine can die.
scenes.turningPointNote: Farrier switches to his reserve fuel tank, beginning the countdown to his fuel running out and establishing the ticking clock for his aerial storyline.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel Winnant watches the blue trawler pushing out to sea low in the water through binoculars, while Commander Bolton watches a destroyer heading out to the Channel where there are shapes of boats on the horizon.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK OF TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Dutch Seaman aims the boat at a destroyer out at the mouth of the harbour.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOLD, TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy, Gibson, Alex, Highlander 1 and the others stuff holes as best they can while makeshift plugs pop out every few seconds and they scrabble underwater to find and replace them with hands pressed against water jets.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Omitted scene.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK OF TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Dutch Seaman looks over the rail concerned to see how fast his boat is lowering into the swell.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: Collins notices oil on the water's surface and warns Mr. Dawson, who puts the screw into reverse to stop the boat, and they fish oil-covered men out of the water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier chases down the Heinkel as its top turret opens fire on him with tracer fire, dives under the rear turret's range, then angles up firing at the bomber's tail.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLUE TRAWLER - DAY
scenes.summary: The Dutch Seaman sees water sloshing over the deck and shouts to abandon ship.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The soldiers holding back water cannot hear the abandon ship order, one by one they abandon the task, Alex and Gibson are last, Alex grabs Gibson's shoulder and jumps for the exit while Gibson notices too late still holding back the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLUE TRAWLER 0 C
scenes.summary: Tommy gets on deck, sees the Keith a quarter mile away, dives into the water pulling away from the swamped trawler, and all the men dive off swimming for the Keith.

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, TRAWLER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Gibson dives for the exit but is blasted back by water and dragged down with the sinking trawler.
scenes.turningPointNote: Gibson is dragged down with the sinking trawler, a devastating loss of the French soldier who had been struggling for survival throughout the film.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter, Collins and oil-covered men pull more survivors from the water filling the yacht's decks, Mr. Dawson orders the oily survivors below deck warning the oil could catch fire, and they comply while Peter runs to shout down the companionway to be careful.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter warns the oily survivors to be careful as they move George's body, and Alex quietly tells him that George is dead.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton sees the civilian armada approaching through field glasses and tells Colonel Winnant he sees home.
scenes.turningPointNote: The arrival of the civilian boats marks the turning point in the evacuation effort — hope arrives.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK OF DESTROYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Exhausted soldiers on the destroyer watch the civilian vessels pass in the opposite direction and begin to clap and cheer, some crying.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter steps out reeling from George's death, the Shivering Soldier asks if the lad will be okay, and Peter nods while Mr. Dawson looks on approvingly.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier strafes the Heinkel with no apparent effect, then dives and banks to line up another shot as a 109 cuts across him with tracer fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. WATER, JUST OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy swims toward the Keith as a Heinkel drops bombs around it, dives underwater for protection, then surfaces to find himself swimming through oil from the sinking blue trawler.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT MALO LES BAINS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The collection of small civilian ships works the beach, picking up men in the shallows and ferrying them to bigger ships while the Engineer watches with pride.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - DAY
scenes.summary: The Moonstone fills with oil-covered men as the Keith lists and men jump off, while Collins watches Farrier bank hard to pursue the Heinkel.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier pulls around hard, gets the Heinkel in his sights, fires his cannons, and the bomber catches fire and starts falling.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, WATER OUTSIDE DUNKIRK HARBOUR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins sees the Heinkel catch fire and yells for Mr. Dawson to go as the flaming bomber falls toward the oil slick, hits the water, explodes, and ignites the surface, forcing men underwater to escape the spreading flames.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier sees the Heinkel explode, turns toward the beaches, observes the thousands of men and the evacuation effort with awe, then hears his engine sputter and die.
scenes.turningPointNote: Farrier's engine dies — he is now gliding without power, fundamentally changing his situation and forcing him to make critical decisions about where and how to land.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton watches civilian boats arrive with satisfaction, waves at Spitfire 1 overhead, then notices the plane has no engine noise.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - EVENING
scenes.summary: The Moonstone chugs along low in the water with men lying across her decks.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Men lie packed in the cabin like sardines as Tommy catches Alex looking at him and nods.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Collins hears a distant ME 109 approaching from the south, and Mr. Dawson climbs up to spot it, instructing Peter to turn the wheel and point the boat south.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton and the soldiers on the mole brace themselves as a Stuka approaches and goes into its dive with its terrible whine building.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - EVENING
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson watches the approaching 109, orders Peter to full speed and prepare to pull hard to port, waiting for the fighter to lower its nose before giving the signal.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - DAY
scenes.summary: Commander Bolton mumbles a prayer as the Stuka comes at them, then Spitfire 1 flashes past and strafes the bomber with gunfire.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier darts past the Stuka while gliding with guns blazing.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Stuka smashes into the sea without firing and soldiers all along the mole cheer.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier watches the Stuka disintegrate on the water's surface, nods, and feels the unaccustomed silence of his powerless plane.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - EVENING
scenes.summary: Mr. Dawson waits for the 109 to commit to its line, shouts now, Peter throws the wheel hard to port, the 109's guns strafe the water to starboard and flash over, then Collins confirms it's gone.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier sits in silence, gliding, looking to see how far he might make it up the beach.

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, MOONSTONE - EVENING
scenes.summary: Tommy gets to his feet and steps over other men as he moves toward the stairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. MOONSTONE, ENGLISH CHANNEL - EVENING
scenes.summary: Tommy and Alex poke their heads out to see the white cliffs, Peter tells them it's Weymouth not Dover, and Alex sadly says they let everyone down.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier glides in the strangely silent plane, banking and looking for a suitable stretch of beach to ditch beyond Malo Les Bains.

scenes.heading: EXT. HARBOUR AT WEYMOUTH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Soldiers disembark at Weymouth harbour, the Shivering Soldier is taken ashore in blankets, Tommy and Alex receive tea, George's body is taken ashore, and a furious soldier confronts Collins about the RAF before Mr. Dawson intervenes.
scenes.turningPointNote: The soldiers reach England — the evacuation is complete for them, shifting from survival at sea to homecoming.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEYMOUTH RAIL YARD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: An elderly blind man hands Tommy and Alex blankets and tea, tells them well done, and when Alex says all they did was survive, the man says that's enough.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tommy flops down and immediately falls asleep while Alex slumps opposite with tears rolling down his cheeks, saying the old man wouldn't look them in the eye.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MOLE - EVENING
scenes.summary: A Private wakes alone on the deserted mole, mistaken for dead, and Commander Bolton calls him down to a launch full of officers, then they look out at the vast deserted beach littered with carnage.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Tommy wakes as the train halts at a siding in Woking, Alex asks a boy for a newspaper, receives it, and begins reading the headline about Dunkirk.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEYMOUTH TOWN - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter walks down the deserted high street and enters the office of the local paper, the Herald.

scenes.heading: INT. HERALD OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Peter hands the Editor a photograph of George.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier checks his canopy is locked, stows loose items, and pumps the handle.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: The landing gear inches out of its housing.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Tommy reads Churchill's speech from the newspaper as the train pulls into the station where cheering civilians crowd the platform with beer, sandwiches, and blankets, and Alex turns from despair to see them celebrating.
scenes.turningPointNote: The soldiers discover they are being welcomed as heroes rather than failures — a fundamental shift in their understanding of their homecoming.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier pumps the handle.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPITFIRE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The landing gear inches past halfway down.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, SPITFIRE 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier pumps the handle, checks his belts, and holds the plane steady in its descent toward the sands.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT LA PANNE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Spitfire 1 swoops onto the flat sand with wheels down, and Farrier slides back the canopy and climbs out of the plane.

scenes.heading: INT. MR. DAWSON’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Peter gets up from the kitchen, Mr. Dawson hands him the Herald showing the headline about George Mills being a hero at Dunkirk, and Peter nods with satisfaction.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT LA PANNE - DAY
scenes.summary: Farrier brushes sand from his Spitfire's wing, pulls his flare gun, and shoots into the cockpit.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex hangs out the window guzzling beer and grinning at women outside while Tommy reads Churchill's speech aloud over the celebration.

scenes.heading: EXT. DUNKIRK HARBOUR - EVENING
scenes.summary: Bodies bob in the water at Dunkirk harbour as abandoned equipment and bodies line the mole in the last light.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT LA PANNE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Farrier kneels with hands on his head as German soldiers surround him and lead him away from the burning plane.
scenes.turningPointNote: Farrier is captured by German forces — his sacrifice is complete, and his fate is sealed as a prisoner of war.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Alex is oblivious to Tommy's reading as Tommy continues the speech to himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. BEACH AT LA PANNE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The camera moves toward the burning Spitfire as Tommy's voiceover completes Churchill's speech, then the flames fill the frame and cut to black.
